chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ طَلَّقَ وَ فَرَّقَ بَيْنَ الشُّهُودِ أَوْ طَلَّقَ بِحَضْرَةِ قَوْمٍ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لَهُمُ اشْهَدُوا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – The one who divorces and separates between the witnesses, or divorces in the presence of a group and does not say to them, ‘Bear witness’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/121)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/121)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ عَلَى طُهْرٍ مِنْ غَيْرِ جِمَاعٍ وَ أَشْهَدَ الْيَوْمَ رَجُلًا ثُمَّ مَكَثَ خَمْسَةَ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ أَشْهَدَ آخَرَ فَقَالَ إِنَّمَا أُمِرَ أَنْ يُشْهَدَا جَمِيعاً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about a man who divorces his wife upon purity, from without copulation, and keeps a man as a witness on that day, then remains for five days, then keeps another. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But rather, he is ordered to have both witnesses together’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who divorces his wife in a Hayd (menses)-free period and without sexual intercourse and asks one man to bear witness on that day, then after five days asks another man to bear witness. He (the Imam) said, ‘He is commanded to arrange witnesses together.”’
